reviews.review_msg: assignments are easy and worth a good portion of your grade however fina and midterm are tough and you need to memerize all processes in textbook. prof was super nice tho and made lectures/online videos intresting. mc quizzes in tutorial every two weeks were hard but not worth much. i would take this course if you need a science/bpk.

Diana had very engaging lectures, and the concepts of the class were interesting and fun to learn. Unfortunately, the tests did not at all encompass what we learned in class. She would test on minor details found in the textbook that were not covered in lecture or mentioned at all throughout the course. She constantly changed deadlines as well.

Amazing prof. Breaks things down in lectures to make it clear for all knowledge levels. Lectures are very well made and engaging. It's clear she has passion for the subject and teaching about it. Very interesting assignments and lessons you'll remember going forward in life even if you aren't pursuing kinesiology. 10/10 would take again.

Never replies to any email. Her videos are ok, but for the midterm and final online, it takes almost an hour to check IDs as it isn't an open book, and you have to be on camera the whole time. Worst experience ever. The videos and slides aren't enough, so get the textbook. She's not very considerate either.

She is so passionate and inspirational I would reccomend this class to anyone. Its a science.. you need to study! Memorizing is a big part of it but also understanding the material. Tests were fair and she gives you assignments that help boost your grade if you dont test so well. Overall amazing prof
so nice and understanding, and takes time for each student regardless of how busy she is!

Tough class. Assignments online are super long, around 100 questions. Tests are extremely difficult because of the time limitation. Only an hour to write exams worth 100+ marks. Crazy. Prof has horrible accent, tries to be funny but isn't. This class should have tutorials, so material could be understood better.

Jon, please dont imagine yourself talking to a bunch of Phil scholars while you are actually informing beginners. Try to express more clearly and slowly, you are not that kind of prof who likes to see 1/3 of innocent students failing with the confusion of unique symbols,r u? An intructor who can simplify a quite difficult course shall be praised!

i did 110 in 2001-2... holy crap! this course is MATH! not phil! and i had extenuating circumstances for a withdrawal and he wouldnt let me out! the dean of phil did, but horban wouldnt. and yet he still to this day says hi to me on campus.... WEIRD GUY!

He is really a good professor, but his exams are not easy. You don't need to attend his class every time cause he posted videos on canvas. Try to organize his notes and ask him when you get confused, you can get good marks.

I took LING 100 and LING 110 with Yourie and he is by far the best ling prof i have had. In both classes a bit of studying is needed, but he is easy to see during office hours and always spends 2 hours before midterms and exams reviewing with the class.

I encourage people to take Dr. Rahman's class; his lectures are like "edutainment" and he's just generally a pleasant person to be in the same room with. HW is light. He's also a very snappy dresser, so you're not giving him a makeover in your mind while he's trying to lecture (not sure if I'm the only one who gets distracted by that thought).

Anis is an awesome guy! very helpful and always encourages questions. His accent is strong and sometimes tough to understand but you will get the concept. Interesting lectures, and he's  got a good sense of humour!
Anis was very funny and was engaged with the course material. The down side was he has a thick accent therefore it was hard to understand what he was saying most of the time.

Scott's lectures could be interesting at times but his vocabulary is way beyond his students (espicially for a first-level class). He tended to lecture on things beyond the scope of the course in order to truly make it a full overview of cmns. 3 Papers at 30% each and 10% for participation makes for an easy class if you can write.
A really good lecturer he is funny and enthusiastic - would 100% recommend! The content was fairly easy just a paper and 2 exams
Took back on 09, very interesting. Great lectures.

This is one of the most boring classes I have ever taken at SFU. CMNS 110 requires a lot of reading, and the reading is super long and dry. His lectures are not always related to the readings too, so it can get a little confusing. He is a very nice guy though, and tells some pretty interesting stories.
Great, clear, helpful and funny, not boring at all.
makes concepts very clear and uses funny stories to make his points!
one of the best profs I ever had....thank you.
